sanskrit: इन्द्रियस्येन्द्रियस्यार्थे रागद्वेषौ व्यवस्थितौ । तयोर्न वशमागच्छेत्तौ ह्यस्य परिपन्थिनौ ॥
hindiTranslation: इन्द्रियइन्द्रियके अर्थमें प्रत्येक इन्द्रियके प्रत्येक विषयमें मनुष्यके राग और द्वेष व्यवस्थासे अनुकूलता और प्रतिकूलताको लेकर स्थित हैं ।  मनुष्यको उन दोनोंके वशमें नहीं होना चाहिये क्योंकि वे दोनों ही इसके पारमार्थिक मार्गमें विघ्न डालनेवाले शत्रु हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Attachment and aversion sit in every sense-object. Do not come under their power, for they are your enemies on the path.
essence: Freedom begins when attraction and aversion stop steering action.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Craving and dislike appear in every sense experience. Do not let either one control your choices, because both block the path forward.
simpleInsight: You are not required to obey every pull and push inside you.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is being taught how to act without being dragged around by inner reactions. Krishna names the two forces that distort judgment: attraction and aversion. Both can sabotage duty before a weapon is even lifted.
modernRelevance: You open a message and feel the instant urge to defend yourself, or to ignore it completely. That split-second pull is exactly what this verse warns against.
translationLiteral: इन्द्रियस्य-इन्द्रियस्य-अर्थे (in each sense's object) / राग-द्वेषौ (attachment and aversion) / व्यवस्थितौ (are established) / तयोः (of those two) / न (not) / वशम् (under the power) / आगच्छेत् (one should come) / तौ (those two) / हि (indeed) / अस्य (for this one) / परिपन्थिनौ (the two enemies on the path)
